title: Volume filter 1.5x on combined portfolio (MR+TF+OG)
hypothesis: Wave 1 proved 1.5x volume filter on MR solo: Sharpe -0.02→0.38, PF 1.30→1.62. Applying to the full combined portfolio should similarly improve signal quality by filtering out low-conviction entries across all strategies.

acceptance_criteria.description: Sharpe improvement >= +0.03 over baseline combined portfolio. Max drawdown increase <= 1pp. Min 150 trades after filtering.

notes: Wave 1 result on MR solo: Sharpe -0.02→0.38, PF 1.30→1.62, DD 5.24%→4.03%, trades 332→235. 1.5x is the sweet spot. 2.0x was too aggressive (115 trades, Sharpe -0.30).

title: Connors RSI(2) mean reversion — solo backtest
hypothesis: Connors RSI(2) with SMA-200 filter has proven 74%+ win rate across 34 years of backtesting (Connors & Alvarez 2008, confirmed by reddit/algotrading 2024). Entry: RSI(2)<10 + close>SMA(200). Exit: close>SMA(5). This is DISTINCT from our existing MR (RSI-14/z-score) and should generate uncorrelated signals on different stocks at different times.

acceptance_criteria.description: Min 100 trades, Sharpe > 0, PF > 1.1, win rate > 55%. RSI(2) strategies typically have high win rates but need enough trades to be statistically meaningful.

notes: New strategy implementation at strategies/connors_rsi2.py. Based on Connors RSI(2) from 'Short Term Trading Strategies That Work'. Uses RSI period=2 (not 14 like existing MR). Exit via SMA(5) crossover.

title: Connors RSI(2) — parameter optimization
hypothesis: Default RSI(2) params may not be optimal for our SP500 top-200 universe. Key params to optimize: rsi_entry threshold (5-15), sma_exit_period (3-10), atr_stop_mult (2.0-4.0), max_hold_days (5-15). Research shows RSI(2)<5 is more selective but higher quality.

acceptance_criteria.description: Optimized Sharpe > 0.30, PF > 1.2, min 80 trades. Must not over-optimize to tiny trade count.

notes: [auto-deferred] rsi2_solo failed: ConnorsRSI2 unprofitable solo. Need tighter stops or higher equity before optimization.

title: Connors RSI(2) — combined portfolio test (MR+TF+OG+RSI2)
hypothesis: RSI(2) generates different signals from existing MR (RSI-14/z-score) at different times, adding uncorrelated alpha. With max_positions=15, there are position slots available. Unlike wave 1 dormant strategies, RSI(2) is designed to be short-duration (avg 3-5 days) so it should not create sustained position contention.

acceptance_criteria.description: Combined Sharpe >= 0.85 (current baseline ~0.87). Max DD <= 7%. Sharpe should not degrade by more than 0.05. If combined Sharpe > baseline, this is a clear win.

notes: [auto-deferred] dependency chain: rsi2_solo→rsi2_opt failed

title: Connors RSI(2) — out-of-sample validation for promotion
hypothesis: RSI(2) combined portfolio improvement survives OOS walk-forward validation. Strategy has 34-year published track record so OOS decay should be minimal.

acceptance_criteria.description: OOS Sharpe >= 70% of IS Sharpe. >50% of walk-forward windows profitable. Perturbation: mean CAGR stays positive after 10 random param shifts.

params_override._note: Use the combined config from wave2_rsi2_combined

notes: [auto-deferred] dependency chain: rsi2_solo→rsi2_opt→rsi2_combined failed

title: Volume filter 1.5x — OOS validation for promotion
hypothesis: 1.5x volume filter improvement on combined portfolio survives OOS validation. This was already OOS-validated on MR solo in wave 1, so combined OOS should also pass.

acceptance_criteria.description: OOS Sharpe >= 70% of IS Sharpe. >50% WF windows profitable. If pass, generate candidate config with volume.min_ratio=1.5 across all strategies.

notes:
If wave2_vol_combined passes AND this OOS passes, create candidate config at config/candidates/sp500_wave2_vol.json. Promote to v2.3 after human approval.

title: Mean reversion — RSI recovery exit vs current MA exit
hypothesis: Research shows MR trades benefit from RSI-based exits over fixed MA exits. When RSI(2) recovers above 65-70, the oversold condition is resolved and holding longer adds risk without reward. Current MR uses SMA(20) reversion + ATR profit target; RSI recovery exit may capture the same edge with less exposure.

acceptance_criteria.description: Sharpe improvement >= 0.05 OR PF improvement >= 0.1 vs current MR exit rules. Min 150 trades.

notes: Connors research: stops HURT mean reversion. Time-based and RSI-recovery exits often outperform fixed stops. QuantifiedStrategies confirms this.

title: Opening gap — IBS-based vs current SMA exit
hypothesis: Opening gap reversal trades should exit when IBS > 0.8-0.9 (close near high = buying exhaustion). This is more targeted than SMA(5) exit. Also test shorter hold periods since gap fills typically resolve in 1-3 days.

acceptance_criteria.description: Sharpe improvement >= 0.05 OR PF improvement >= 0.1 vs current OG exit. Min 80 trades after filtering.

notes: Gap fills are fast events. Shorter holds reduce exposure risk.

title: Trend following — Chandelier exit vs current trailing stop
hypothesis: Chandelier exit (highest-high minus N×ATR) adapts to volatility better than fixed trailing stop ATR multiplier. It locks in profits during strong trends while giving room during pullbacks. Testing different ATR multipliers (2.5-4.0) against current trailing_stop_atr_mult=2.5.

acceptance_criteria.description: Sharpe improvement >= 0.05 vs current TF exit. Chandelier should capture more trend profit with adaptive stops.

notes: Current TF: trailing_stop_atr_mult=2.5, max_hold=15. Research suggests wider stops (3-4x ATR) capture more trend.

title: Turn of Month timing filter on combined portfolio
hypothesis: The Turn of Month effect (last 5 + first 3 trading days) shows stocks generate virtually all monthly returns in this window (Lakonishok & Smidt 1988, confirmed 2024). Boosting signal confidence during TOM window (or suppressing signals mid-month) should improve trade quality. This is a calendar-based filter, completely uncorrelated with our price-based signals.

acceptance_criteria.description: Sharpe improvement >= 0.02 when only taking trades in TOM window vs taking trades anytime. Min 100 trades to avoid under-trading.

notes: Quantpedia, QuantifiedStrategies, and academic research (Etula et al 2020) confirm TOM effect is robust across 34+ countries and 100+ years. Mechanism: pension fund rebalancing + payroll cash flows at month-end.

title: IBS entry filter sweep on MR (combined mode)
hypothesis: Requiring low IBS (close near day's low) for MR entries improves signal quality. Alvarez research shows IBS < 25 gives 58% avg gain improvement on RSI(2) strategy. Our MR has ibs_max=1.0 (disabled). Testing restrictive thresholds should filter out weak MR signals.
notes: From Alvarez Quant Trading & Pagonidis IBS research. IBS < 0.25 is the sweet spot.

title: Volume min_ratio sweep on MR (combined mode)
hypothesis: Higher volume threshold for MR entries improves trade quality. Wave 1 proved 1.5x volume on MR solo: Sharpe -0.02→0.38, PF 1.30→1.62. Wave 2 combined test FAILED due to infrastructure bug (nested params). This experiment uses full volume dict sweep to bypass the nested param issue. Expect 1.5x to be optimal in combined mode too.
notes: Retry of wave2_vol_combined with full-dict sweep to bypass nested param bug.

title: Triple RSI — solo backtest on SP500
hypothesis: Triple RSI (RSI(5) declining 3 days, below 30, with lookback check) generates rare but high-conviction mean reversion signals on individual SP500 stocks. Published edge on SPY: 90% WR, PF 4.0. Adapted for individual stocks with SMA-200 filter and volume confirmation. Expects fewer but higher-quality trades than existing MR strategy.
notes: Strategy implemented in research/strategies/triple_rsi.py (sandbox). Solo metrics unreliable at $4K — focus on trade count, WR, PF for viability.

title: Triple RSI — parameter optimization
hypothesis: Coordinate descent on Triple RSI parameters will find optimal balance between selectivity and trade count. Key tensions: shorter RSI period = more signals but noisier; fewer decline days = more trades but lower quality; tighter stop = less drawdown but more whipsaws.
notes:
If solo test shows < 30 trades, reduce decline_days to 2 or lower rsi_entry.
[analyst-deferred] Dependencies not passed: ['wave3_trsi_solo=failed']

title: Triple RSI — combined portfolio test
hypothesis: Triple RSI generates rare signals (estimated 30-80/year) that won't compete significantly with MR/TF/OG for position slots. Unlike dormant strategies that generated 300-700 trades and degraded combined Sharpe, Triple RSI's low trade count should have minimal position contention. Combined portfolio may see slight Sharpe improvement from diversification.
notes:
CRITICAL TEST: All 4 previous dormant strategies failed combined test due to position contention. Triple RSI's low trade count is designed to avoid this failure mode.

title: MR with best IBS + volume filters in combined mode
hypothesis: Stacking the best IBS threshold from wave3_ibs_sweep AND the best volume threshold from wave3_vol_sweep will compound their benefits. Each filter independently improves signal quality; combined effect should be multiplicative (better entries = higher PF, lower DD). Risk: over-filtering may reduce trade count below useful threshold.
notes:
If IBS and volume sweeps both pass, manually construct params_override with best IBS + best volume values before running this test.

title: Full SP500 portfolio reoptimization (post-filter + TRSI)
hypothesis: After adding IBS filter, volume filter, and (if passed) Triple RSI, the portfolio parameters may be suboptimal. Full coordinate descent reoptimization should find the best parameter combination across all active strategies. Expect Sharpe improvement from current v2.2 baseline.
notes:
Run AFTER filter stacking test. If Triple RSI combined test also passed, enable it in config before reoptimization. Otherwise reopt with MR+TF+OG only.

title: OOS + perturbation validation of reoptimized config
hypothesis: Reoptimized config must survive out-of-sample validation before promotion. Three-test suite: (1) time-split OOS Sharpe > 0, (2) perturbation ±15% stability > 50%, (3) walk-forward window win rate > 50%. All three must pass per lesson #6.
notes:
GATE: No promotion without OOS pass. Use validate_oos.py with candidate config.

title: RSI period sweep on MR (combined mode) — RSI(5) vs RSI(14)
hypothesis: Web research (Triple RSI, Connors, Alvarez) consistently shows RSI(2-5) outperforming RSI(14) for mean reversion signals. Our MR uses RSI(14). Shorter RSI periods may improve entry timing. Combined-mode sweep gives realistic portfolio-level impact unlike unreliable solo sweeps (lesson #30).
notes: From web research: RSI(2) and RSI(5) are the standard for MR strategies. If a shorter period wins, it could be a simple config change for immediate profit.

title: max_hold_days sweep on MR (combined mode)
hypothesis: Wave 2 tested max_hold_days in SOLO mode (all negative Sharpe due to fee drag at $4K). Relative ranking showed 10 > 15 > 7 > 5 > 3. Combined-mode sweep gives realistic absolute Sharpe. Short holds (3-5d) may reduce time risk; longer holds (10-15d) capture more reversion.
notes: Lesson #30: Solo param sweeps unreliable at $4K. Combined mode is required. Current default is max_hold_days=10 (from v2.2). May confirm or change.

title: Lower Band Reversion solo test — published params on SP500 stocks
hypothesis: The Quantitativo IBS lower-band strategy (Sharpe 2.11 on SPY) can generate profitable signals when adapted to individual SP500 stocks. Published params: range_lookback=25, high_lookback=10, band_mult=2.5, IBS<0.3, exit on close>prev_high.

acceptance_criteria.notes: Pass if generates sufficient trades with signal quality. PF>0.8 for first pass (untuned).

notes: Based on published research: Quantitativo (2024), Pagonidis (2013). SPY results: Sharpe 2.11, 69% WR, PF 1.98.

title: Lower Band Reversion solo — relaxed IBS threshold for more trades
hypothesis: Relaxing IBS threshold from 0.3 to 0.5 generates more trades on individual stocks (which have wider IBS distributions than SPY). Tests if the band signal alone carries enough edge without strict IBS filtering.

acceptance_criteria.notes: More trades expected; accept if PF>0.7 (optimization will tighten later).

notes: Testing if broader IBS threshold generates enough trades for individual stocks.

title: Lower Band Reversion full optimization — coordinate descent
hypothesis: Coordinate descent over LBR parameters will find optimal settings for individual SP500 stocks (which differ from SPY ETF). Key params: band_mult, high_lookback, range_lookback, ibs_threshold, atr_stop_mult.

acceptance_criteria.notes: PASS if optimized Sharpe>0.20 and PF>1.1. Strong enough solo to proceed to combined test.

notes: [auto-deferred] LBR solo unprofitable (Sharpe -2.08, PF 0.85, edge p=0.25). No point optimizing.

title: Lower Band Reversion combined portfolio test — add to MR+TF+OG
hypothesis: LBR signals are uncorrelated with existing MR (RSI+zscore) because they use different entry logic (price band + IBS vs oscillator). Adding LBR to the portfolio should improve Sharpe without position contention because LBR trades are infrequent (high-conviction entries).

acceptance_criteria.notes: PASS if combined Sharpe >= baseline and DD increase < 2pp. PATTERN: 4 prior strategies failed here due to position contention at max_pos=10 (now 15).

notes: [auto-deferred] LBR optimization skipped — solo strategy unprofitable.

title: Lower Band Reversion out-of-sample validation
hypothesis: LBR optimized params hold up in out-of-sample period (last 20% of data). OOS Sharpe should be > 50% of IS Sharpe to confirm robustness.

acceptance_criteria.notes: PASS if OOS Sharpe > 50% of IS Sharpe. Confirms edge is not overfit.

notes: [auto-deferred] LBR dependency chain failed — strategy unprofitable.

title: MR max_hold=5 out-of-sample validation — Wave 3 finding
hypothesis: Wave 3 found max_hold=5 beats max_hold=10 (Sharpe +0.035, CAGR +3.1pp, PF 4.55 vs 3.64). OOS validation needed before promotion. MR trades resolve quickly — 5-day hold captures most reversion, longer holds add noise.

acceptance_criteria.notes: PASS if OOS confirms max_hold=5 is better than current max_hold=10.

notes: From wave3_hold_combined: Sharpe 0.6424, CAGR 30.86%, PF 4.55 with max_hold=5. Needs OOS before promotion.

title: MR exit rule: close > prev high — borrowed from LBR research
hypothesis: The LBR published exit rule (sell when close > yesterday high) captures the first sign of strength recovery. Testing this on existing MR strategy as an alternative to the current profit-target + mean-reversion exit. Expected: faster exits, higher win rate, possibly lower avg profit per trade.

acceptance_criteria.notes: PASS if Sharpe within 0.05 of baseline. Even neutral is useful if WR improves.

params_override.notes: Testing shorter holds to approximate strength exit behavior

notes: Cross-pollination: applying LBR exit research to existing MR strategy.

title: LBR band_mult sensitivity sweep — how wide should the band be?
hypothesis: Band multiplier controls selectivity: wider band = fewer but deeper dips. Published used 2.5x. Testing 1.5-4.0x to find optimal trade-off between trade frequency and signal quality on individual stocks.

acceptance_criteria.notes: Informational — find optimal band_mult for individual stocks.

notes: Published 2.5x was for SPY. Individual stocks may need different band width.

title: LBR IBS threshold sweep — how strict should the IBS filter be?
hypothesis: IBS threshold controls signal quality vs quantity. Published used 0.3 for SPY. Individual stocks have different IBS distributions. Testing 0.1-0.6 to find optimal threshold that maximizes risk-adjusted returns.

acceptance_criteria.notes: Informational — find optimal IBS threshold for stock universe.

notes: Key research from Alvarez: IBS<25 gives 21% increase in avg return for MR strategies.

title: LBR ablation: without SMA-200 filter — does deep-dip entry work in downtrends?
hypothesis: SMA-200 filter was a clear win for existing MR (+0.28 Sharpe). But LBR specifically targets extreme dips — which often occur in downtrends. Testing if removing SMA-200 captures more deep-dip opportunities that still revert quickly. Published strategy on SPY used SMA-300 as improvement.

acceptance_criteria.notes: PASS if no-filter Sharpe > filtered Sharpe. Otherwise confirms SMA-200 is beneficial.

notes: VACUOUS PASS — 0 acceptance criteria. Both variants unprofitable (filter_off Sharpe=-1.44, filter_on Sharpe=-2.08). Informational: removing SMA-200 is less bad for LBR (PnL -$4.52 vs -$174.35). NOT promotion-worthy.

title: Full SP500 portfolio reoptimization — re-tune params for SMA-200 era
hypothesis: SMA-200 filter (promoted v2.1) fundamentally changed the trade mix (443→270 trades). All MR/TF/OG parameters were optimized WITHOUT SMA-200 active. Re-running coordinate descent with SMA-200 enabled should find better parameter combinations. ASX reopt in Wave 1 yielded +0.17 Sharpe improvement from a similar post-filter reopt.
notes: Baseline: Sharpe 0.87, CAGR 11.7%, DD 5.3%. Last reopt was pre-SMA200.

title: OOS validation of reoptimized SP500 config
hypothesis: Reoptimized parameters from wave5_full_reopt maintain edge on unseen out-of-sample data, confirming the improvement is genuine and not overfitting.
notes: Promotion candidate if passes. Compare to current v2.2 OOS baseline.

title: MR profit_target_atr_mult sweep (combined mode) — optimize take-profit
hypothesis: Current MR profit target (1.5x ATR) was set pre-SMA200. With the trend filter active, winners may run further (in uptrend). A higher profit target could capture more profit per trade. Sweeping 1.0-3.0x in combined mode to find optimal take-profit level.
notes: Previous wave3 max_hold sweep showed Sharpe +0.035 for shorter holds. Profit target is the complementary parameter.

title: TF trailing_stop_atr_mult sweep (combined mode) — optimize trailing stop width
hypothesis: TF trailing stop (currently 2.5x ATR) determines how much room trends have to breathe. With SMA-200 filtering out downtrends, surviving trades are higher quality and may benefit from tighter or wider stops. Sweep 1.5-3.5x in combined mode.
notes: Wave2 chandelier_tf tested stop widths in solo mode (misleading). This tests combined mode.

title: OG gap_threshold sweep (combined mode) — tune gap sensitivity
hypothesis: Opening gap only generates 9 trades over the backtest period with current thresholds. The -1.5% gap threshold may be too strict with SMA-200 filtering. Relaxing gap threshold to -1.0% or -0.5% could increase trade count while maintaining quality.
notes: OG had only 9 trades in Wave 2 exit test. Need more trades for any conclusion. SMA-200 may allow relaxed thresholds.

title: Allocation pool A/B test — validate pools don't degrade current portfolio
hypothesis: Enabling allocation pools (TF:5, MR:5, OG:3) with current 3 active strategies should produce results within 5% of no-pools baseline. Pool totals (13) are under max_positions (15), so no crowding. This validates the pool system works correctly before adding strategies.
notes: Critical gate: if pools degrade, the pool system has bugs. Current pools: TF:5, MR:5, OG:3, MB:5, _other:2.

title: Consecutive Down Days — solo backtest on SP500 (new strategy)
hypothesis: Buying large-cap stocks after 3+ consecutive down closes in uptrends (above SMA-200) captures short-term reversal premium. Academic research (Quantpedia, Groot et al. 2012) shows this effect generates 30-50 bps/week net of costs on large caps (Sharpe ~1.09 in published results). Signal is fundamentally different from RSI-based MR — counts close-to-close direction rather than oscillator levels.

acceptance_criteria.note: Solo metrics are secondary at $4K equity (fee drag). Primary check is trade count and PF direction.

notes: New sandbox strategy at research/strategies/consecutive_down_days.py. Default params: min_down_days=3, SMA-200 on, IBS<0.3, strength exit (close > prev high), max_hold=5, ATR stop 2.0x. Pattern: solo tests are misleading at $4K but validate the signal generates enough trades.

title: Consecutive Down Days — parameter optimization (coordinate descent)
hypothesis: Default CDD params are from published research (index-level). Optimizing min_down_days, ibs_threshold, atr_stop_mult, and max_hold_days via coordinate descent should improve metrics for SP500 individual stock universe.
notes: Only run if solo test produces 100+ trades. Grid has 4^4=256 combos but coord descent is ~16 evals.

title: Consecutive Down Days — combined portfolio test (MR+TF+OG+CDD)
hypothesis: CDD signal is uncorrelated with RSI-based MR (different signal source: consecutive closes vs oscillator level). Adding CDD to the portfolio should improve Sharpe through diversification without excessive position contention. If CDD generates fewer trades than MR (~100 vs 270), contention should be manageable even without allocation pools.
notes: PATTERN: All 4 prior dormant strategies failed combined test due to position contention. CDD may succeed because: (a) fewer trades than MB/SR/STMR, (b) uncorrelated signal timing. If it fails, retry with allocation pools enabled.

title: Consecutive Down Days — OOS validation for promotion
hypothesis: Optimized CDD params from wave5_cdd_opt hold up on unseen out-of-sample data, confirming the edge is real and not overfitted.
notes: Promotion candidate if combined test also passes. Both must pass for promotion.
